reviews.review_title: Cool but gets boring kind of fast
reviews.review_description: I like the freedom of the cooking part of the game but it is still kind of limited in some aspects. There are only two maps they're basically the same but they just have different layouts and looks. I feel like the actual cooking part is kind of complicated but some recipes are still fun to make. It NEEDS multiplayer I feel like it would be so much Fun to just cook with your friends. Overall if you want a basic cooking game I recommend but don't get your hopes too high definitely not life-changing. 😂

I haven’t unlocked any more recipes after multiple in game days. My fame is 150/150. The guest complaints are messed up. A recipe calls for 5mg salt. I put 5mg salt in the recipe and the complaint is “not enough salt” and “too much salt”. In the same cooked meal! Please tell me this is a glitch. Also, how can I possibly take a 1kg beef chuck and “shave” off 100 grams. As precise as I try to be, I end up cutting 95g or 103 gram. Now I can’t get the 5 star rating for the dish. It’s enough to drive me crazy. I want to really like this game. But some of the mechanics are not reliable. Food just drops through the plates. It’s really annoying repeatedly cooking the same handful of recipes over and over with never unlocking a new one. If I could get a refund on this game, I would. Do the developers even read these reviews???

reviews.review_title: I've been waiting for more than a year
reviews.review_description: Plsss continue on the dlcs plsss

Cooking Simulator VR, in short, is a good game. What makes it such a good game is the vast amount of creativity given to the player, especially in its Sandbox mode.
As stated before, the amount of creativity Cooking Simulator VR has is what makes it such an incredible game, especially with its built-in Sandbox mode. You have the freedom to serve a bowl of water with plate shards in it. You have the freedom to spawn infinite eggs and water bottles and blow them up with gasoline. It’s all to your decision.
One small issue Cooking Simulator has is the lack of food items it has. Although a small issue, it still makes the game a little more boring. Even though there’s not that much food, that should not stop someone from enjoying the game.
In summary, Cooking Simulator’s greatness lies within its creativity. Although it lacks food, someone can still enjoy the game. The game player doesn’t even have to cook food in order for the game to be enjoyable. Remember that this is an opinion, and all opinions should be taken with a grain of salt. Not every player has the same experience and/or opinion. Otherwise, an excellent game.
This is a pretty bad game I would like a refund
Everytime I click on the game it would load for 2 seconds and then the screen goes black.

This game deserves to have 5 stars but... you need to add  these things. 
1. More food like rice, flour, dough, chocolate, baking soda, ice cream, marshmallows, cheese slices, butter, ice, gelatin, bottom of a bun, icing, sprinkles, ribs, yogurt, cherries, peaches, lettuce, spinach, cake and cupcake stuff, cabbage, pineapple, watermelon, and blueberries.
2. More liquids.
3. More spices.
And last but not least ADD MULTIPLAYER PLEASE!!!

This game is AMAZING. It has given me amazing moments that no other game has come close to. It has this unique contrast of calm therapeutic gameplay, compared to the comedic “do what you want” aspect of it all. Say you want to make a burger. You can spend time grilling the meat to perfection, or just shove it in the microwave and hope guests don’t notice. The added feature of relax mode takes away the timer on each order, giving more of a slow, steady, and perfectionist feel. The default mode, is basically just “how many dishes can you cook all at once” and it is absolutely hilarious. I agree with some other reviews that the addition of a multiplayer mode would be an huge awesome update, but it’s just that, huge. It would be quite difficult to create a proper multiplayer mode with this game, but who knows, maybe one is already in development. Maybe by the time you read this it’s already a feature. There are a few physics based bugs, but that is to be expected with any vr physics game. Side note: I’ve only encountered said bugs when I intentionally tried to bug the game, they don’t shine through during normal gameplay. If a member of the dev team is reading this, i have stumbled on a glitch: if you take the radio (for fear of softlocking the game I haven’t tested it with anything else) and stand on top of it, then pick it up and raise it up while still standing on it, you fly upwards and out of the map. To give credit where it’s due however, the game’s boundary detection was very good at putting me back in bounds in a matter of seconds. If there is a single thing I would like to see added to this game, maybe some of the items found in the DLCs from the PC version of the game. Maybe you could have multiple possible kitchens for those types of cooking/baking. In summary, this game is a 5 star in my book. The devs have cooked up a masterpiece of a game, and I’ve had a blast playing it. Definitely  recommended.

reviews.review_description:
Addendum #2 You still have not fixed the issue of getting dropped from the game after finishing a level and before entering the next day. So this means I've lost my level and have to replay it. I am going to delete the game and request a refund. Addendum: The game keeps crashing on me right after I finish a level and just before it auto saves. In career mode, I will get to the end where I'm selecting where to spend my skill points and choosing a recipe and bam! the game crashes on the save. Its not happening everytime but often enough to where i';m about to ask for my money back. I'm tired of having to go back and redo a level. 
Other bugs are as follows:
a) When I manually save the game it will boot me off the game, everytime, this is frustrating. 
b) the mechanics for the utensils is just annoying. I cannot use the spatula because all it does is push the food around and make a mess, I end up using the tongs or my hands for flipping the food. 
c) when you finish a round in the career mode your given the option to select your perks and/or spend your points. Once this is done your only option is to start the next day. I like to spend my points then be given the option of saving and/or returning to the main menu or quitting the game. Instead what I have to do is start the next day and then pause and leave the game.

Even though this game is rated E, it's not great for kids. The game is too similar to real life so it can be hard for a kid's brain to distinguish between VR and real life afterward. They might feel like they are "trapped in VR".

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your feedback. We understand your concern about the potential impact of virtual reality on children. The game is rated E for everyone and has been thoroughly tested for safety and appropriateness for children. As always, it's important to monitor children's use of any form of technology and to have open and honest conversations with them about their experiences. Thank you again for your comment.

Kacper | Gameboom VR
